title: Context Selection, Compression, Deduplication, and Position · Guided Program Studio
summary: A retrieval engineer and domain partner incrementally build citation-mapped context assembler, change one query, document, tenant, language, model, index, or policy condition, and keep earlier Python, provenance, accessibility, security, evaluation, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of citation-mapped context assembler while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Allocate tokens across instructions, query, history, evidence, citation metadata, answer, and safety reserves before assembly.
Collapse duplicate and overlapping chunks while retaining source diversity, conflicting versions, amendments, and dissent evidence.
Compare extractive selection, sentence filtering, summaries, and structured compression against retained claim and citation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rag Context Token Budget
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop RAG engineering team handles case 9045 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to deduplicate, compress, order, and citation-map context within a measured token budget; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Allocate tokens across instructions, query, history, evidence, citation metadata, answer, and safety reserves before assembly.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Context Citation Map
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-context-citation-map-15ywfb" that uses case 9231 to deduplicate, compress, order, and citation-map context within a measured token budget. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Rag Context Citation Map.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-context-citation-map-15ywfb
# Competency: rag-context-citation-map.
# Case 9231: an enterprise retrieval migration canary.
# Operating constraint: keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_context_citation_map_15ywfb(candidate_tokens: int = 6400, context_budget: int = 2200, selected_tokens: int = 1800, duplicate_chunks: int = 1, citation_links: int = 6, order_tested: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_15ywfb = "15ywfb-bfwy51-9231"
    assert evidence_variant_15ywfb
    evidence_axes_15ywfb = (
        "corpus15ywfb",
        "querybfwy51",
        "tenant9231",
        "index15ywfb9231",
        "promptbfwy519231",
        "evaluation923115ywfb",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_15ywfb)) == 6
    if min(candidate_tokens, context_budget, selected_tokens) < 0:
        return False, selected_tokens, "token-accounting"
    if selected_tokens > context_budget or context_budget > candidate_tokens:
        return False, selected_tokens, "context-budget"
    if duplicate_chunks < 0 or citation_links < 1:
        return False, citation_links, "context-identity"
    if not order_tested:
        return False, citation_links, "position-bias"
    retained = selected_tokens - duplicate_chunks * 100
    assert retained >= 0
    return True, retained, "citation-mapped-context"
